creator: Anthony Ha
title: (No AI) OpenAI’s existential questions
dc:creator: Anthony Ha
content: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.
contentSnippet: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) The 12-month window
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.
contentSnippet: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

title: (No AI) Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Vercel homepage with the tagline “build and deploy on the AI cloud”](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/Screenshot-2026-04-19-at-3.53.46PM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data. A person claiming to be a member of [ShinyHunters](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/vercel-confirms-breach-as-hackers-claim-to-be-selling-stolen-data/amp/), which was behind the recent hack of [Rockstar Games](https://www.theverge.com/games/910815/rockstar-games-says-hack-will-have-no-impact), posted some data online, including employee names, email addresses, and activity time stamps. Vercel confirmed in a post on X that a "security incident" had occurred, and that it impacted a "limited subset" of its customers. Vercel said that a compromised third-party AI tool was the avenue for attack, though it did not specify which third party was involved.

> We've identified a security incident that inv …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/914723/vercel-hacked)
contentSnippet:
Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data. A person claiming to be a member of ShinyHunters, which was behind the recent hack of Rockstar Games, posted some data online, including employee names, email addresses, and activity time stamps. Vercel confirmed in a post on X that a "security incident" had occurred, and that it impacted a "limited subset" of its customers. Vercel said that a compromised third-party AI tool was the avenue for attack, though it did not specify which third party was involved.


We've identified a security incident that inv …


Read the full story at The Verge.

A person claiming to be a member of ShinyHunters, which was behind the recent hack of Rockstar Games, posted some data online, including employee names, email addresses, and activity time stamps. Vercel confirmed […]

title: (No AI) The RAM shortage could last years
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Circuitboard with error message.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/12/STKS523_RAM_SHORTAGE_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

According to [_Nikkei Asia_](https://asia.nikkei.com/business/tech/semiconductors/memory-shortage-set-to-run-until-2027-as-chipmakers-focus-on-ai), even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until [2030](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/south-koreas-sk-group-chairman-expects-chip-wafer-shortage-last-until-2030-eyes-2026-03-16/).

The world's largest memory makers - Samsung, SK Hynix, and [Micron](https://www.theverge.com/news/847344/micron-ram-memory-shortage-2026-earnings) - are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.

_Nikkei_ says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to [_Counte …_](https://counterpointresearch.com/en/insights/the-global-memory-shortage-will-cost-us-all)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/914672/the-ram-shortage-could-last-years)

The world's largest memory makers - Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron - are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.
Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counte …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research

creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth
content: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.
